questions.question: Name three pioneering cloud service providers and the year they launched their first major cloud service.
keyPoints: Amazon Web Services (AWS) – launched S3 and EC2 in 2006

questions.question: Explain the role of virtualization in enabling cloud computing models.
keyPoints: Virtualization partitions physical hardware into multiple isolated virtual machines
Allows on‑demand allocation and elastic scaling of resources
Provides multi‑tenancy by isolating users on shared infrastructure
Forms the foundation for IaaS and PaaS offerings

questions.question: Discuss the historical development of cloud service providers, highlighting key milestones and the emergence of major providers such as AWS, Azure, and Google Cloud. Also explain how service models (IaaS, PaaS, SaaS) evolved alongside these providers.
keyPoints: Early concepts: grid computing (1990s) and utility computing (early 2000s) laid the groundwork
AWS launched commercial IaaS in 2006 (S3, EC2), marking the first public cloud services
Google App Engine (2008) introduced Platform‑as‑a‑Service; Microsoft Azure (2010) offered a hybrid IaaS/PaaS suite
Service models evolved from IaaS (e.g., EC2) to PaaS (e.g., App Engine, Azure App Service) to SaaS (e.g., Salesforce, Google Workspace)

questions.question: Compare and contrast the deployment models (public, private, hybrid, community) in the context of the evolution of cloud computing. Provide examples of how each model has been adopted by enterprises historically.
keyPoints: Public cloud – shared resources provided by a third‑party (e.g., AWS, Azure, Google Cloud)
Private cloud – dedicated infrastructure for a single organization (e.g., IBM Cloud Private, VMware vSphere‑based clouds)
Hybrid cloud – combines public and private clouds, enabling data/app portability (e.g., Azure Stack, AWS Outposts)
Community cloud – shared infrastructure for a specific industry or group (e.g., government‑run cloud platforms, healthcare cloud consortia)
